CPL Initiative — Project Dashboard

Linked to Vision 2030 | CPL Workplan | CPL Budget Plan
▶ Project Description
The CPL Initiative is a statewide effort led by the California Community Colleges Chancellor's Office to expand Credit for Prior Learning opportunities for veterans, working adults, and apprentices across all 116 colleges. Aligned with Vision 2030, the initiative builds AI-enhanced infrastructure, faculty-led credit recommendations, student-centered data systems, and strategic partnerships to ensure prior learning is recognized and applied toward degree completion and career advancement. The initiative has served over 43,000 students, transcribed 97,000+ units, and established the foundation for a permanent statewide CPL service.
📎 See Attachments
Last Updated: June 01, 2026 at 2:53 PM PT

Cumulative CPL Students
Target: 250,000 by 2030
Military24,150
Workforce/Other22,082
Apprentice749
How this is calculated
Source: CCCCO MAP CPL Dashboard (live scrape via Cloudflare worker).
Formula: Direct value pull of the STUDENTS SERVED metric.
Assumptions: Breakdowns (Military / Workforce-Other / Apprentice) are reported by CCCCO directly; not recomputed.
Caveats: Falls back to the Excel KPI metric on project 3.1 if the live scrape is unavailable.

Source: MAP CPL Dashboard
Military109k
Workforce/Other101k
Apprentice6k
How this is calculated
Source: CCCCO MAP CPL Dashboard (live scrape).
Formula: Direct value pull of the ELIGIBLE UNITS metric.
Assumptions: Population breakdowns mirror the CCCCO dashboard.
Caveats: Falls back to the Excel KPI metric on project 3.2 if the live scrape is unavailable.
Description last updated: 2026-04-19
96k
Transcripted Units
Target: 85,000 (exceeded)
Military13k
Workforce/Other83k
Apprentice6k
Applied Credits (posted to student records)205,078
How this is calculated
Source: CCCCO MAP CPL Dashboard (live scrape).
Formula: Direct value pull of the TRANSCRIBED UNITS metric. 'Applied Credits' is a secondary number posted to student records.
Assumptions: Transcribed = credits actually posted to a student record (distinct from 'eligible').
Caveats: Population breakdowns are reported directly by CCCCO.
Description last updated: 2026-04-19
11,152
Credit Recommendations
Source: MAP Custom Reporting Module
CCC Collaborative (statewide faculty workgroups)1,257
Local (individual college articulations)9,895
Standardized Assessment: 5,779
Industry Certification: 3,257
Credit By Exam: 1,536
Portfolio Review: 380
Military: 112
How this is calculated
Source: MAP Custom Reporting Module (CustomReport_latest.json → View_ArticulatedMAPExhibits).
Formula: Count of articulation rows across all colleges (each row = one college-to-course articulation).
Assumptions: Each row represents a distinct (Exhibit, College, Course) mapping.
Caveats: Known issue: the raw export contains some duplicates, so the CR count can exceed the implied product of exhibits × colleges. The underlying MAP Articulation Analysis section also inherits these duplicates.
Description last updated: 2026-04-19
2,456
MAP Exhibits
71 originating colleges
CCC Collaborative (statewide exhibits)139
Local (college-created exhibits)2,317
How this is calculated
Source: MAP Custom Reporting Module (View_ArticulatedMAPExhibits).
Formula: Distinct count of ExhibitID values across the dataset.
Assumptions: ExhibitID is the primary key for an exhibit (same exhibit articulated at multiple colleges counts once).
Caveats: 'Originating colleges' (62) counts colleges that created exhibits, not colleges that adopted them.
Description last updated: 2026-04-19
63
CCC Collaborative Adoption
Colleges adopting statewide exhibits
Collaborative Exhibits139
Collaborative Credit Recs1,257
How this is calculated
Source: MAP Custom Reporting Module (View_ArticulatedMAPExhibits).
Formula: Distinct count of colleges that have adopted at least one exhibit whose Collaborative Type contains 'CCC'.
Assumptions: Adoption = a row exists where the college articulates a CCC Collaborative exhibit to a local course.
Caveats: Counts adopting colleges only, not colleges that created collaborative exhibits.
Description last updated: 2026-04-19
98
Active Colleges
of 116 system colleges
Leading Colleges (meeting at least 3 of 5 criteria)14
Advancing Colleges (active, building capacity)84
Inactive Colleges (<10 students and 0 units)17
1. Student Volume ≥ 500
2. Eligible Units ≥ 3,000
3. Avg Eligible Units/Student ≥ 5
4. Transcription Rate ≥ 25%
5. Avg Transcribed Units/Student ≥ 3
How this is calculated
Source: CCCCO MAP CPL Dashboard (tier classification computed by the Cloudflare worker).
Formula: Leading + Advancing tiers. A college is Leading if it meets ≥3 of 5 criteria, Advancing if 1-2, Inactive otherwise.
Assumptions: Criteria: Students ≥ 500; Units ≥ 3000; Avg Units/Student ≥ 5; Transcription Rate ≥ 25%; Avg Transcribed/Student ≥ 3.
Caveats: Thresholds live in cloudflare-worker-proxy.js (lines 184-188). Editing the KPI_Config sheet updates this description but does NOT automatically redeploy the worker.
Description last updated: 2026-04-19
102
Articulating Colleges
Colleges with MAP exhibits
Originating Colleges (created exhibits)71
Adopting CCC Collaborative (statewide exhibits)63
How this is calculated
Source: MAP Custom Reporting Module (View_ArticulatedMAPExhibits).
Formula: Distinct count of colleges appearing in the 'Articulation College' field.
Assumptions: A college is 'articulating' if it appears as the receiving college on at least one exhibit row.
Caveats: 'Originating Colleges' (62) = colleges that created exhibits; 'Adopting CCC Collaborative' (55) = subset that adopted statewide exhibits.
Description last updated: 2026-04-19
$293M
Estimated Savings
Eligible units, Beacon Economics
Military$156M
Workforce/Other$144M
Apprentice$9M
How this is calculated
Source: CCCCO MAP CPL Dashboard (live scrape). Fallback: $269M from KPI_Config sheet.
Formula: Live value pull. CCCCO computes savings using the Beacon Economics per-unit factor (≈ $1420/unit).
Assumptions: Per-population breakdowns (Military/Workforce/Apprentice) are proportional splits by eligible-unit share, not independent calculations.
Caveats: BEACON_PER_UNIT_SAVINGS is a reference value; the actual savings math happens at CCCCO.
Description last updated: 2026-04-19
50
VETERAN SPRINT
Star Colleges
JST Credits24,150 / 30,000
Basic Training Credit50 Colleges
Eligible CPL109k Units
How this is calculated
Source: Live CCCCO MAP CPL Dashboard scrape (star_college_count). Augmented with live military data from the same scrape.
Formula: Headline = live star_college_count from the CCCCO scrape. JST Credits = military students (from cumulative_students breakdowns). Eligible CPL = military eligible units (from eligible_units breakdowns).
Assumptions: Goal for JST Credits = 30,000 at Veteran Star Colleges.
Caveats: Headline value is the number of colleges participating, not students served. Pulled live from the CCCCO MAP CPL Dashboard each pipeline run.
Description last updated: 2026-04-19
$1.17B
20-YEAR IMPACT
Source: MAP CPL Dashboard
Military$624M
Workforce/Other$578M
Apprentice$37M
How this is calculated
Source: CCCCO MAP CPL Dashboard (live scrape).
Formula: Direct pull of the 20-YEAR IMPACT metric. CCCCO applies a 4.0x multiplier to one-year impact.
Assumptions: Beacon Economics long-term earnings model; breakdowns by population are proportional splits.
Caveats: The multiplier lives on the CCCCO side — changing TWENTY_YEAR_MULTIPLIER here is for documentation only.
Description last updated: 2026-04-19
📈 KPI Trends Academic Q4 2026  ·  QTD resets each academic quarter (Jul/Oct/Jan/Apr)
Tracking since 2026-04-14  ·  49 daily snapshots
MetricToday1d7d30dQTD1yr30-Day Trend
CPL Students46,156▲0.1%▲0.7%▲2.1%——
↳ Military 24,150▲0.1%▲0.8%▲2.7%——
↳ Workforce / Other 22,082▲0.1%▲0.6%▲1.5%——
↳ Apprentice 749—▲1.4%▲2.6%——
Credit Recs11,152—▲1.3%▲44.3%——
Active Colleges98——▲1——
Est. Savings$293M—▲0.5%▲2.0%——
MAP Exhibits2,456—▼889▼756——
CCC Collaborative63—————
Articulating Colleges102——▲13——
Veteran Star Colleges50——▲1——
How this is calculated
Source: kpi_history.json — one snapshot appended per day (idempotent; same-day runs overwrite).
Formula: For each metric, render: today's value, deltas at 1d/7d/30d/QTD/1yr, and a 30-day sparkline.
Assumptions: QTD resets at the start of each academic quarter (Jul/Oct/Jan/Apr). Deltas are % for volumetric metrics and absolute counts for Active Colleges, MAP Exhibits, CCC Collaborative, Articulating Colleges, and Star Colleges.
Caveats: A dash (—) means no historical value exists yet for that period; tracking begins the first time the pipeline ran. Sparklines need 2+ snapshots to render.
Description last updated: 2026-04-19
🏆 College Activity
Leading Advancing Inactive As of 2026-06-01
Tier ★ College District Students Veterans Working Apprentices Eligible Units Transcribed Exhibits Credit Recs Disciplines Savings 20-Yr Impact Trans. Rate Criteria Last Activity
● Criteria met:   1. Students≥500  ·  2. Units≥3k  ·  3. Avg≥5  ·  4. Trans≥25%  ·  5. AvgTrans≥3 ⭐ MAP Star College (≥1 criterion) Last Activity: ● ≤30d ● 31–90d ● 90+d
How this is calculated
Source: CCCCO MAP CPL Dashboard via Cloudflare worker — per-college tier classifications.
Formula: Same 3-of-5 criteria as Active Colleges. Leading (≥3 met), Advancing (1-2 met), Inactive (<10 students and 0 units). Tiers sorted by criteria met desc, then by students desc.
Assumptions: Criteria: Students ≥ 500; Units ≥ 3000; Avg Units/Student ≥ 5; Transcription Rate ≥ 25%; Avg Transcribed/Student ≥ 3.
Caveats: ⭐ indicates MAP Star College status (at least one criterion met). JST column derives from View_StudentAggregatedValues rows where Military Credits > 0.
Description last updated: 2026-04-19
CPL Analytics Source: MAP Custom Reporting · Generated 2026-06-01T21:53:17 ▲
CCC Collaborative vs. Local Exhibits
Statewide faculty workgroup articulations vs. individual college articulations
⬇ Excel
CategoryCredit RecsExhibitsCollegesDisciplines%
Local9,6703,575992386.7%
CCC Collaborative1,25716363811.3%
Industry/Other2254422112.0%
Total11,1523,782——100%
How this is calculated
Source: View_ArticulatedMAPExhibits (MAP Custom Reporting Module).
Formula: Group rows by Collaborative Type. Count distinct exhibits, rows (credit recs), colleges, and disciplines per group.
Assumptions: Categories: Local (college-specific), CCC Collaborative (statewide faculty workgroup), Industry/Other (anything else).
Caveats: An exhibit has exactly one Collaborative Type. '%' is each group's share of total credit recs.

Other882780.8%
Total11,1523,782—100%
How this is calculated
Source: View_ArticulatedMAPExhibits.
Formula: Group by CPL Type. Count distinct exhibits, rows (credit recs), and colleges per type.
Assumptions: Six CPL types: Standardized Assessment, Industry Certification, Credit By Exam, Portfolio Review, Military, Other.
Caveats: Each exhibit belongs to exactly one CPL type.

10 modes
⬇ Excel
Mode of LearningCredit RecsExhibitsColleges%
Self-study, exam preparation, other (S)6,7341,8129260.4%
Industry training (I)2,5189227322.6%

High school coursework (H)260217172.3%

Total11,1523,782—100%
How this is calculated
Source: View_ArticulatedMAPExhibits.
Formula: Group by Mode of Learning code (S/I/A/H/N/M/O/G/P/J). Count exhibits, rows, colleges.
Assumptions: Mode taxonomy defined by CCCCO; each exhibit carries one MoL code.
Caveats: Codes with very low counts (e.g. Justice-involved learning) reflect limited adoption, not missing data.

How this is calculated
Source: View_ArticulatedMAPExhibits.
Formula: Group by CCC Discipline. Count exhibits, rows (credit recs), courses, colleges, and CCC Collaborative adoptions.
Assumptions: 'Not Mapped' = exhibits without a discipline tag.
Caveats: ~31% of credit recs are Not Mapped — this flags a tagging backlog, not missing articulations.

Total102 colleges11,0638,309—1,2493,238100%
% = each college's share of the 11,152 total credit recommendations statewide
How this is calculated
Source: View_ArticulatedMAPExhibits.
Formula: Group rows by Articulation College. Count credit recs (rows), distinct exhibits, disciplines, CCC Collaborative rows, and Industry Certs.
Assumptions: Each row represents one college-to-course articulation of one exhibit.
Caveats: Credit Recs > Exhibits is expected: one exhibit can articulate to multiple courses at the same college. Known inflation from duplicates in the raw export.

How this is calculated
Source: View_ArticulatedMAPExhibits.
Formula: Group rows by ExhibitID, sum credit recommendations across all colleges, sort desc, take top 50.
Assumptions: Ranking = total articulation count statewide.
Caveats: Ties broken by exhibit ID order. Counts reflect raw row counts and can include the known duplicate inflation.

—34 hours in TrigonometryTrigonometry (+1)53
Earned articulations grouped by unified course identity. Adoption leverage = peer colleges teaching the same course that have not yet earned it. 119 over-merged identities show leverage as ⚠ flagged (withheld). Full set (2,355 identities) in the Excel export.
How this is calculated
Source: kb/coci_articulations.json — earned MAP articulations (View_ArticulatedMAPExhibits) resolved to the unified course-identity layer.
Formula: Group earned articulations by unified course identity (C-ID/CCN when the row carries a CID Number, else a minted M-ID; multi-M-ID rows disambiguated by local Course Title). Per identity: count colleges that earned it, the modal credit recommendation, linked credential(s), and adoption leverage = peer colleges teaching the same identity that have not yet earned the articulation.
Assumptions: One identity = one common course across colleges. Adoption leverage is a candidate list, not a guarantee — title-consistent membership only.
Caveats: GE-area articulations are excluded (routed separately). Adoption leverage is suppressed (shown as flagged) for identities marked over_merged, since the cluster may conflate distinct courses and would yield bogus adoption targets.
Description last updated: 2026-05-22

Vision 2030 Alignment

CPL Initiative alignment with Vision 2030 Actions 1a & 5, and the three CPL system goals — with live progress against each.

Latest UpdateAB 123 chaptered ($5M ongoing + $15M one-time); Title 5 updates advanced; $2M + $35M proposed in Governor's 2026 budget

CPL Workplan Progress — Path to 2030

Solid lines = actuals · Dashed lines = projected · Right-click any chart to copy image

Stretch Trajectory (500K Target)

Total Military Workforce/Other Apprentice — Solid = actual   - - - Dashed = projected
🛠 Element Map

Projects (34)

Project data as of 2026-06-01

Goal 1 Expand Equitable CPL Access & Boost Student Success 13 projects
By 2030, expand CPL opportunities for at least 250,000 Californians, including 220,000 working adults and apprentices, and 30,000 veterans.
  • Offer or award CPL using 1,000 statewide credit recommendations from 25 faculty/industry workgroups
  • 80% of CPL recipients report improvements in savings, belonging, well-being, completion, and career prospects
  • 500 personal success stories documented in MAP
Stretch: Serve 500,000 learners (450K adults + 50K veterans); 3,000 credit recommendations from 40 workgroups; 90% reporting improvements; 1,000 success stories
Statewide Credit Recommendations
Contributes to: ★ Activity 2 ✎
Create and adopt statewide CPL credit recommendations through ASCCC faculty workgroups.

Goal 2 Build a Unified, Interoperable, Student-Centered CPL System 17 projects
By 2030, build a unified statewide CPL system with CPL-embedded outreach pathways, interoperable data, and seamless student experiences.
  • MAP platform serving all 116 colleges with AI-enhanced tools and credential matching
  • Full interoperability with CCCApply, eTranscript, MIS, and Credential Engine
  • Statewide CPL data infrastructure tracking offers, awards, transcription, and student outcomes

Add and refine MAP features to support student-centered CPL, enhance user experience, security, and functionality to create and maintain a one-stop CPL infrastructure for the system.

Goal 3 Sustainable Policies, Resources & Professional Learning 4 projects
By 2030, establish clear CPL policies, sustainable resources, professional development, and best practices across all colleges.
  • Permanent CPL operational funding secured ($5–7.5M ongoing)
  • Title 5 §55050 regulatory updates reducing residency and access barriers
  • 1,000+ staff, faculty, and administrators trained in CPL implementation
  • Scalable technical assistance model for all 116 colleges
Law & Regulation Review
Contributes to: ★ Activity 4 ✎
Advance improvements to laws, regulations, procedures, and constituent resolutions focused on removing CPL barriers are enacted
On Track
Progress 60%
Lead: Samuel Lee / James Todd
Team: CO Policy Staff, Legislative Affairs

Timeline: 2024-07-01 – —
KPI: 4 policy actions
Milestones: 2026 session: Advocate for $2M ongoing + $35M one-time; advance SCFF integration

Last updated: 2026-04-07
Latest UpdateAB 123 chaptered ($5M ongoing + $15M one-time); Title 5 updates advanced; $2M + $35M proposed in Governor's 2026 budget
Workplan NoteAB 123 chaptered establishing first-ever ongoing CPL state funding ($5M ongoing + $15M one-time). Title 5 regulatory updates advanced to reduce residency and access barriers. Draft trailer bill for additional $2M ongoing + $35M one-time under review.

Vision 2030 Alignment

Vision 2030 Action 1a

Develop infrastructure to ensure that multiple pathways to a baccalaureate degree are available to all students, including completion via the community college system, including expansion of CPL and credential recognition.

Vision 2030 Action 5

Advance equitable and transparent credentialing systems within and across the California higher education systems that recognize both traditional and competency-based learning.

Expand Equitable CPL Access & Boost Student Success

By 2030, expand CPL opportunities for at least 250,000 Californians, including 220,000 working adults and apprentices, and 30,000 veterans.

  • Offer or award CPL using 1,000 statewide credit recommendations from 25 faculty/industry workgroups
  • 80% of CPL recipients report improvements in savings, belonging, well-being, completion, and career prospects
  • 500 personal success stories documented in MAP
Credit Recs: 1115.2% (11,152/1,000)  |  Students: 18.5% (46,156/250,000)

Build a Unified, Interoperable, Student-Centered CPL System

By 2030, build a unified statewide CPL system with CPL-embedded outreach pathways, interoperable data, and seamless student experiences.

  • MAP platform serving all 116 colleges with AI-enhanced tools and credential matching
  • Full interoperability with CCCApply, eTranscript, MIS, and Credential Engine
  • Statewide CPL data infrastructure tracking offers, awards, transcription, and student outcomes
Active Colleges: 98 of 116 (84%)

Sustainable Policies, Resources & Professional Learning

By 2030, establish clear CPL policies, sustainable resources, professional development, and best practices across all colleges.

  • Permanent CPL operational funding secured ($5–7.5M ongoing)
  • Title 5 §55050 regulatory updates reducing residency and access barriers
  • 1,000+ staff, faculty, and administrators trained in CPL implementation
  • Scalable technical assistance model for all 116 colleges
1,000+ trained; $5M ongoing secured; Title 5 updates advancing
⚠️ Beta / draft — AI-assisted course unification, pending human review. Per-row quality flags (over-merge, mixed-attribute, unreviewed) are shown so you can vet the data.

Common Course Reference — a cross-college course identity reference for faculty review

Consolidated course identities (M-IDs) and variant clusters with discipline, credit status, units, TOP code, and cross-college articulation adoption. Adopted = colleges that earned a CPL articulation on the course; Adoptable = colleges teaching a like course that haven't yet (hover a number for college names). Stand-Alone (single-college) courses aren't listed as rows here — they're in the Excel export, and searchable in the ⚇ Unify dialog to consolidate.

Common Subjects Reference Development Draft curator-edited
— one shared 4-letter abbreviation per discipline

This is a development draft — nothing here is final. Different colleges abbreviate the same discipline different ways. Sign Language American shows up across 10 different codes (ASL, AMSL, DEAF, SIGN, …); Physical Education across 71. This page picks one 4-letter code per discipline so the dashboard uses a single, consistent label everywhere. Click the Common SUBJ cell on a row to set or change a code, add notes if you'd like, then a faculty validator confirms when you're done.

Scope. Common Subject Codes are used for CPL crosswalk purposes only — they let colleges find and adopt each other's articulation decisions without getting tangled in subject-code variations. They are not a claim of equivalence with CCN, CID, or any official credentialing system. Discipline names on this page come directly from the CCC 2025-26 Minimum Qualifications Handbook — the authoritative source for who's qualified to teach what in California Community Colleges.

Variants

Curation guidelines

This page is curator-edited. Here's the simple logic for picking a Common Subject Code.

1. Why 4 letters?

We follow the same shape as California's Common Course Number (CCN) format: a 4-letter subject + a number. Choosing 4 letters keeps our codes visually consistent with the CCN family that's rolling out statewide, even though our codes are clearly marked as ours (we use an M prefix instead of CCN's C).

2. How were the suggestions chosen?

For each discipline, we counted up how many of the local college subject codes match each 4-letter pattern and proposed the most common one as the default. If that "most common" code is shorter than 4 letters (PE, KIN, BUS, etc.), the row stays blank — a curator needs to pick a 4-letter expansion.

3. Choosing a code

  • Prefer an established abbreviation faculty already recognize (e.g. BIOL, MATH, ENGL).
  • If multiple short codes are common, pick one that reads naturally (PHYE for Physical Education, BUSI for Business).
  • Use the Variants column to see how colleges currently code the discipline — those numbers reflect real usage.
  • The Notes field is for explaining a non-obvious choice (e.g. "ASL is the established 3-letter abbreviation; expanded to ASLN to fit the 4-letter rule.").

4. Validating

Once you've set a code, the row shows pre-seeded or reviewed. A second faculty reviewer can click Validate to mark the row as faculty-approved. Both roles are restricted to allowed CCCCO MAP reviewers.

5. The 4-letter rule

Every Common SUBJ code must be exactly 4 letters A–Z. The system will reject blanks, lowercase, numbers, and any other length.

Questions? Reach out via the MAP team.

Common Exhibit Reference Development Draft

Curator surface for the credential-identity layer (kb/unified_titles.json + kb/credentials.json).

Each row is a unified credential identity collapsed from many freehand MAP exhibit titles (e.g. EMT Certification unifies 31 raw title variants). The AI-classified unified_title, issuing_agency, and confidence scores come from the exhibit-canonicalization skill. Mark initiated records that you've reviewed the classification — it doesn't change the underlying data; the daily generator picks the curated state up via the Supabase overlay. Audit chips (⚠) show how many audit rules fire on a row's raw variants — hover to see the specific tags. Edits gated on the allowed-reviewers list; sign-in is shared with the Common Course Reference tab.

Exhibit Adoption & Credit Recommendations

Statewide CPL exhibit adoption — each unified exhibit credential with its CPL type, collaborative type, modal credit recommendation, and the colleges that have earned the articulation. Moved out of CPL Analytics into its own tab for room to work.

Pipeline — M-ID lifecycle, audit health, and phase progress

A progress board for the Common Course Reference identity work. Tracks (1) where each M-ID currently sits in the staging → MC-submission → CIDx-approval pipeline, (2) the auditor's cleanup-receipt invariants for the most recent re-mint, and (3) status across the multi-phase roadmap.

Phase roadmap

From CLAUDE.md §11 — the M-ID → CIDx pipeline. Each phase advances trust in a row's data without claiming transferability (M-IDs are MC, not TMC).

loading…

M-ID lifecycle

How a synthetic course identity matures from a Phase-B seed into an ASCCC-approved C-ID. Counts pulled from the latest auditor run.

loading…

Auditor cleanup-receipt invariants

Live tag counts from kb/row_audit/latest.json. The cleanup-receipt invariants (top row) are the audit rules that should fall to zero at the end of a re-mint cycle.

loading…

Most recent re-mint — Phase 1e (SUBJ4 canonicalization)

The atomic apply that folded 65,311 M-ID identifiers onto curator-confirmed canonical SUBJ4s. Shipped 2026-05-23 in commit 5406055. subject_collision_signal auditor flags fell 7,203 → 0.

loading…
Diagrams rendered via Mermaid. Tab loads the library on first activation. Source: pipeline.js.

CPL Budget Support Letters

Curator surface for the May 2026 Revise RC support-letter campaign. Edits the shared letter blocks (statewide, joint, individual) that generate-letter assembles into per-invitee DOCX downloads. Read-only by default; a curator passcode unlocks edits. New letter campaigns can be drafted at + New Letter.

📖 Claude + Knowledge Base — Quick Start

How to start a Claude Code session that reads the CPL Knowledge Base — safely.

✅ The 3 steps

  1. Go to claude.ai/code and start a New session (the “+”).
  2. Repository: choose cpl-initiative/cpl-knowledge-base.
    Don’t leave this blank. Blank means the session has nothing to read. Picking this repo is the one thing that loads the knowledge base.
  3. Environment: leave it on the default (cloud) environment — no change needed.

Then just type your question. That’s it.

🔒 Will I mess anything up just by asking?

No. Asking, reading, searching, and summarizing change nothing on the real knowledge base.

Each session works on a private, throwaway copy. The real cpl-knowledge-base changes only if Claude makes an edit and opens a Pull Request and that PR is merged — three deliberate steps. The main branch is protected, so a session can’t overwrite it.

👉 Browse and ask freely — you can’t break it.

✍️ When you DO want to add knowledge

Just say so, for example: “Add a note about [topic] to the knowledge base and open a PR for it.” Claude puts the change on a branch and opens a Pull Request — nothing goes live until it’s reviewed and merged.

💡 Good to know

  • The knowledge base has a CLAUDE.md file and a docs/ folder that Claude reads automatically — you don’t need to point it at specific files.
  • Example openers: “What do we know about [topic]?” · “Summarize the notes on [topic].”
TL;DR — New session → pick the cpl-knowledge-base repo (not blank) → keep the default cloud environment → ask away. Reading never changes anything; adding knowledge happens only through a Pull Request you ask for.
